speaker: patient
text: Good morning, doctor. I've been feeling a bit off lately and wanted to check in.

speaker: doctor
text: Good morning. Can you tell me more about what you've been experiencing? Any new symptoms or concerns?

speaker: doctor
text: Based on your history, I think it’s a good time to start you on Lisinopril 10mg once daily to help manage your blood pressure.

speaker: patient
text: Alright, I can start that. Should I take it in the morning or at night?

speaker: doctor
text: You can take it in the morning with breakfast to keep it consistent.

speaker: doctor
text: By the way, your recent labs show your hemoglobin is at 139 grams per liter, which is within normal range.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic blood pressure today is 79 mmHg, which is quite good.

speaker: patient
text: That sounds okay, but I’m still a little worried about some dizziness I’ve been feeling.

speaker: doctor
text: I see. Your systolic blood pressure was measured at 137 mmHg earlier, which is mildly elevated.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, that dizziness has been bothering me more frequently, especially when I stand up quickly.

speaker: doctor
text: Interestingly, a more recent reading shows your systolic pressure has dropped to 117 mmHg, which might explain the dizziness.

speaker: doctor
text: Given this, I’m going to adjust your treatment and start you on Insulin glargine 10 units at night to better control your blood sugar.

speaker: patient
text: I understand. Will this new insulin affect my blood pressure as well?

speaker: doctor
text: Insulin primarily helps with blood sugar, but controlling diabetes can indirectly improve your overall cardiovascular health.

speaker: patient
text: Should I monitor my blood pressure more frequently now?

speaker: doctor
text: Yes, I recommend checking your blood pressure daily and noting any symptoms like dizziness or lightheadedness.

speaker: doctor
text: Your latest hemoglobin level, however, has increased to 161 grams per liter, which is higher than before.

speaker: patient
text: Is that something to be concerned about? What could cause the increase?

speaker: doctor
text: Elevated hemoglobin can indicate dehydration or other underlying conditions; we should investigate further with additional tests.

speaker: patient
text: But I thought my hemoglobin was 139 grams per liter the last time we checked?
